title: Autocomplete hard-negative rank auxiliary

queries: hard negative ranking loss multiclass candidate ranking
candidate ranking auxiliary loss MRR multiclass classification

evidence.why_now: EXP108 showed that the raw-label / prefix-style baseline surface can preserve the salt-side item-incoterms ranking signal near the strict baseline, while EXP129, EXP156, and EXP159 showed that replacing or lightly residualizing that surface still leaves item-incoterms clearly worse. EXP126 also showed that autoregressive shortlist reranking is too expensive for routine screening. The next justified step is therefore to keep the cheap O(C) candidate-score surface and add a direct hard-negative ranking objective aligned with MRR, rather than adding another scorer-replacement or residual-scale variant.

hypothesis: If item-incoterms is failing because cross-entropy over candidate logits does not put enough pressure on the hardest wrong candidate under the cheap scorer, then adding a margin-free hard-negative ranking auxiliary should improve MRR-style ordering while preserving the scalable Stage 4-compatible sample-candidate scoring interface. The mechanism changes the supervision signal, not the residual scale, token-stat feature set, prompt packaging, sampling, or pretrain path.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, sampling, packaging, or route-aware conservative losses.
This candidate does not change autocomplete_decision_interface and does not use candidate_pairwise_ar_hybrid or any O(L*C) scorer.
The hard-negative auxiliary is applied only for autocomplete multiclass tasks; user-churn and user-ltv should act as no-op drift controls.
The auxiliary loss is margin-free softplus(hardest_negative_score - true_label_score), so this first screen does not manually scan a new margin or residual scale.
Use packed_batch_equivalent scheduling to preserve each task's effective global batch while improving GPU utilization.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.

Resolved packed batch-equivalent placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPUs 0,5 with per-rank batch 2 in wave 0; user-ltv -> lab25211 GPUs 6,7 with per-rank batch 1 in wave 0; item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPUs 0,5,6,7 with per-rank batch 1 in wave 1.
First post-launch status check showed EXP162 and EXP163 windows up in startup / DB-load; EXP164 is correctly waiting for wave 1; orchestrator recommendation: keep_watching.
Completed on 2026-05-09 with global verdict: failed.

EXP162 user-churn is abnormal rather than scientific evidence: it received SIGTERM before the first evaluation on lab25211 GPUs 0,5, with local_rank 0 corresponding to physical GPU0 under CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,5.
EXP163 user-ltv completed with mae=76.62298232233618, which is neutral under the wide replay-aware effective delta.

EXP164 item-incoterms completed with mrr=0.7555898718252234, better than recent collapse plateaus but still clearly worse than strict baseline mrr=0.8147880873466811 under the 0.013037038915945098 effective threshold.
Because item-incoterms is decisively worse, do not rerun EXP162 just to recover the missing churn control.
Conclusion: the hard-negative auxiliary changed the salt-side failure mode upward, but not enough to make the default candidate-score surface promotable.
